deployedGeneratedSources.name: #utility.yul

typeDescriptions.typeString: mapping(string memory => struct ProductRegistry.Batch storage ref)
mapping(string memory => struct ProductRegistry.Batch storage ref)

documentation.text:
Store a batch's Merkle root. In a real system you'd guard this so only
 the manufacturer can call it (OpenZeppelin's Ownable / AccessControl) —
 we'll keep it open here to stay focused on the Merkle part.

typeDescriptions.typeString: mapping(string memory => struct ProductRegistry.Batch storage ref)
mapping(string memory => struct ProductRegistry.Batch storage ref)

documentation.text: Read back a stored root (handy for the UI / sanity checks).

typeDescriptions.typeString: mapping(string memory => struct ProductRegistry.Batch storage ref)
mapping(string memory => struct ProductRegistry.Batch storage ref)

documentation.text:
THE HEART OF THE CONTRACT.
 Given a product's leaf + its proof, rebuild the root and compare it to
 the stored root. Returns true (genuine) or false (fake/tampered).
 `view` = read-only, costs no gas when called off-chain. Identical inputs
 and output to tree.verify(proof, leaf, root) from Steps 3 & 4.

getRoot(string).notice: Read back a stored root (handy for the UI / sanity checks).

registerBatch(string,bytes32,uint256).notice: Store a batch's Merkle root. In a real system you'd guard this so only the manufacturer can call it (OpenZeppelin's Ownable / AccessControl) — we'll keep it open here to stay focused on the Merkle part.

verifyProduct(string,bytes32[],bytes32).notice: THE HEART OF THE CONTRACT. Given a product's leaf + its proof, rebuild the root and compare it to the stored root. Returns true (genuine) or false (fake/tampered). `view` = read-only, costs no gas when called off-chain. Identical inputs and output to tree.verify(proof, leaf, root) from Steps 3 & 4.

userdoc.notice: Stores ONLY the 32-byte Merkle root of each batch on-chain, and         verifies individual products against it using a Merkle proof.         This is the on-chain twin of our JavaScript scripts:           - registerBatch() stores the root (like Step 2's output)           - verifyProduct() checks a leaf + proof (like Step 3 / Step 4)
